no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-e568e815 — no further changes required.

What is done:
- The FEAT-019 contraction described in docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md
  (lines 96–99) is already merged in commit 2950c2ca ("docs(feat-019): contract to evaluation UX;
  workflow shapes → skills [ddx-e568e815]"), which is an ancestor of base-rev 3198c48a.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md now scopes FEAT-019 to the evaluation UX
  layer: comparison record storage and display (req 1–6), grading rubric storage/display (req 7–10),
  grading invocation (req 11–16), benchmark result aggregation (req 17–19a), bead-session linkage
  (req 20–21), and replay result display (req 22–25).
- Workflow shapes (compare-prompts, replay-bead, benchmark-suite) are explicitly delegated to the
  skills library in Overview, Functional Requirements preamble, CLI Interface, and Out of Scope.
- Peer-to-FEAT-010 framing is removed: the spec now states "child of FEAT-010 (exec substrate) —
  not a peer" (line 23) and reiterates the parent/child relationship at lines 45 and 53. The two
  legacy peer assertions referenced by the plan (former :40 and :404) no longer exist in the file.
- FEAT-010 is present in the depends_on frontmatter (line 8).

Why no commit on this attempt:
- The previous attempt for this bead (review-flagged) appears to have produced execution metadata
  without applying the spec edit, but the underlying spec edit had already been committed upstream
  (commit 2950c2ca) and is included in the current base-rev. There is no remaining diff to make
  against the spec; producing an empty or cosmetic commit would not satisfy any AC item and would
  pollute history.

What a re-review needs:
- Re-evaluate the bead against the current state of docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md
  (commit 2950c2ca) rather than against the prior attempt's empty diff. The contraction is in place.
